headline: PENDING — diverse-ensemble agreement MACHINERY only; no two-MODEL-family judge run on a decontaminated set.

standalone.note: Offline demo (tools/ensemble_agreement_study.py --demo) runs two real DETERMINISTIC distinct-family authorship verifiers (temporal date-arithmetic vs provenance corpus). It demonstrates the agreement + error-correlation + AND-consensus machinery on real verifiers: the families miss DIFFERENT misattributions, so AND-consensus catches what either single verifier lets through. This certifies the MACHINERY, not a model-judge capability.

criticalFailures: no_model_judges: the powered arm needs >= 2 distinct MODEL families (judge != subject), plugged via tools/ensemble_agreement_study.py --judge-model; the offline arm uses deterministic verifiers only
no_decontam_run: the scored authorship items must pass tools/assert_decontam.py against the judges' training signal
no_agreement_ci: Cohen kappa / Gwet AC1 and the consensus-vs-single difference need bootstrap CIs over a real labeled set
breaker_not_asserted: the canary circuit-breaker must be ARMED and passing for a powered run (guardrail)
requiredResponse: Add a second distinct MODEL-family verifier (tools/ensemble_agreement_study.py --judge-model <spec> on the farm, Actions-gated), measure inter-judge kappa + error-correlation on a decontaminated authorship set, and promote only if AND-consensus false-approval is below the best single with a CI excluding 0 AND the judges' errors are decorrelated. High error-correlation is a NO-GO even at high agreement.
